description: Build APK on PR and Upload as Comment - Automatically build a debug APK for every pull request, upload it as an artifact, and post a PR comment with a download link

userStories.title: Add Android build job to CI workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the CI to build an Android APK on every PR so that build-breaking changes are caught before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: A new job 'build-apk' is added to .github/workflows/ci.yml
The job runs on pull_request events only (uses a conditional to skip on push events)
The job sets up JDK 17 using actions/setup-java@v4 with cache: gradle
The job sets up Node.js 20 using actions/setup-node@v4 with npm cache
The job runs 'npm ci' to install JS dependencies
The job runs './gradlew assembleRelease' from the android/ directory
The build uses the existing debug signing config (no secrets needed)
The workflow YAML is valid and parseable
All quality checks pass

userStories.title: Upload APK as GitHub Actions artifact
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the built APK uploaded as a workflow artifact so that I can download it from the Actions UI.
acceptanceCriteria: A step using actions/upload-artifact@v4 uploads android/app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk
The artifact is named 'app-release-apk'
The artifact has a retention period of 7 days
The upload step is part of the build-apk job after the Gradle build step
All quality checks pass

userStories.title: Post PR comment with APK download link
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want a comment on the PR with a link to the APK artifact so that I can quickly download and test the build on a device.
acceptanceCriteria: After the APK is uploaded, a comment is posted on the PR with a link to the workflow run artifacts page

If a comment from the bot already exists on the PR, it is updated instead of creating a duplicate (use peter-evans/create-or-update-comment@v4 or equivalent with a hidden marker comment for identification)
The comment includes the commit SHA for traceability
The comment uses a clear, readable markdown format
The workflow has appropriate permissions: contents: read, pull-requests: write
All quality checks pass
